frameworks.name: persistent attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: stable population patterns sustain choice representation across time

frameworks.name: transient sequence-based dynamics
frameworks.one_line: brief sequential activity claimed to encode choice over time

frameworks.name: random connectivity models
frameworks.one_line: diverse single-neuron responses arising from near-random recurrent wiring

frameworks.name: topographic cortical organization
frameworks.one_line: response-field topography as scaffold for learned task variables

core_question: How are choices represented across space and time in primate prefrontal cortical populations?
key_assumptions: large-population recordings can resolve spatial and temporal structure of choice signals
single-neuron response diversity reflects underlying circuit structure
persistent vs sequential dynamics are distinguishable in population activity
manipulations: epoch around choice (delay period vs saccadic execution)
spatial sampling across the cortical surface
key_findings: diverse choice responses reduce to a few mostly persistent population patterns
patterns are recruited at progressively later times around the choice
brief sequential activity appears during saccade but is absent in the delay
response-field topography across cortex explains much of the response diversity
interpretations: PFC choice coding is largely persistent, not sequence-based
spatial topography forms a fixed scaffold on which context-dependent task representations are learned
